title: Cognitive styles diversity and group creativity: Evidence from fNIRS hyperscanning.

assets.abstract: This study explores how cognitive style diversity (differences in information processing among team members) influences group creativity through interpersonal neural mechanisms. 116 college students were assigned to high-diversity (HD) groups (mix of field-independent and field-dependent individuals) or low-diversity (LD) groups (homogeneous cognitive styles). Both groups completed a creative task (umbrella design) and a routine task (desk purchase) while their prefrontal cortex (PFC) and right
